costDrivers: repository-wide discovery was needed once to locate existing token, review, retrospective, and automation docs
avoidableCosts: future tasks should start from PROJECT_EXPERIENCE_GUIDE.md and PROJECT_REVIEW_SYSTEM.md instead of broad retrospective scans
productLessons: project retrospectives are governance and execution learning, not PRD authority
technicalLessons: append-only jsonl keeps task retrospectives cheap to add and easy to aggregate
designLessons: UI/UX lessons should capture interaction and visual evidence quality, not only token cost
processLessons: weekly summaries should read review docs and the ledger, then promote only repeated lessons
tokenLessons: token usage must be recorded as a cost signal but interpreted against task value and outcome
avoidNextTime: do not reduce project retrospectives to token optimization only

costDrivers: first retrospective intentionally performed a broad repository document scan
recent review statistics required inspecting review filenames and repeated evidence phrases
avoidableCosts: future weekly reviews should start from docs/retrospectives/weekly/2026-W28.md and only inspect new reviews since the previous retrospective
adjacent visual-only UI work should be batched into owner-visible bundles instead of creating a full review cycle for every small surface change
productLessons: the useful docs are the routing docs and failure retrospectives, not additional parallel plans
technicalLessons: P6 retrospectives remain the best anti-pattern checklist for evidence and integration design
designLessons: UI/UX polish needs bundle-level foreground acceptance planning to avoid repeating smoke-vs-foreground disclaimers
processLessons: review cadence should distinguish micro-implementation slices from meaningful checkpoint packages
tokenLessons: a wide scan was justified once to build the source map, but repeating it would be low value
avoidNextTime: do not rescan all historical docs for ordinary implementation tasks
do not let high review-file count substitute for visible product closure

costDrivers: first expanded baseline intentionally scanned project history from inception, docs inventory, review inventory, loop history, stage reports, product docs, research docs, and recurring review patterns
avoidableCosts: future historical grounding should start from docs/retrospectives/PROJECT_BASELINE_RETROSPECTIVE.md instead of rescanning the whole repository
ordinary implementation tasks should load only authority docs, the lane doc, the experience guide, and one or two matching historical retrospectives
productLessons: write owner resets into authority docs quickly and isolate old lanes so historical scope does not leak back into the active product surface
technicalLessons: keep source-of-truth contracts, preview/export semantics, binary validity, visual playback, and owner acceptance as separate states
designLessons: batch adjacent visual-only UI/UX polish into surface-level acceptance bundles with one foreground evidence path
processLessons: review volume can become a cost center when every tiny neighboring change gets its own full review cycle
large milestones should be owned by vertical user flows rather than technical layers
tokenLessons: a broad scan has high value once for baseline creation but low value if repeated as routine task setup
avoidNextTime: do not treat review-count growth as product progress
do not reload every historical document when a baseline router already exists

costDrivers: 15 Figma screenshot reads were needed to archive stable visual targets for the main short-term UI states
rate-limit spacing added elapsed time but avoided read-limit and retry risk
productLessons: Figma design screenshots are implementation targets and evidence inputs, not PRD authority or Owner acceptance
technicalLessons: rendered screenshot dimensions can include window/shadow treatment and differ from first inventory frame dimensions
designLessons: contact-sheet visual QA quickly confirms that a screenshot archive covers the intended state set before UI code changes
processLessons: separate R1 screenshot evidence from later token and component reads so quota attribution and task review stay clean
tokenLessons: pilot-first screenshot capture can prevent quota waste when archive/download assumptions are unproven
avoidNextTime: do not batch many Figma screenshots before proving URL download and local hash recording work

costDrivers: initial all-token Figma read was truncated and required split collection reads
mapping had to compare 95 Figma variables against the existing 480-definition CSS token layer
avoidableCosts: future Figma variable extraction should split by collection from the start
productLessons: Figma tokens are implementation inputs and do not override the PRD
technicalLessons: the current CSS token layer has many component tokens but lacks Figma-aligned base palette tokens and full spacing/radius scales
designLessons: high-fidelity visual polish should start after base and semantic token drift is resolved deliberately
processLessons: keep R2 documentation-only so truncation recovery and token-map review stay clean
tokenLessons: return compact values from Figma variables: hex colors, alias IDs/names, and no unnecessary codeSyntax or key fields
avoidNextTime: do not request all Figma variables with full metadata in one response

costDrivers: Owner clarification required revising R3 and R4 before more Figma reads
the existing plan still treated component reading as a flat component index plus component contract list
avoidableCosts: future component reads should not start from flat component-library scans when an atomic hierarchy exists
productLessons: component hierarchy changes implementation read strategy but does not change short-term product scope
technicalLessons: Figma component reads should capture stable contracts and dependencies rather than every descendant layer
designLessons: module-first reading preserves atomic design composition and reduces irrelevant atom and molecule reads
processLessons: Owner MCP authorization gates should be written into the read plan before calls are made
tokenLessons: classify hierarchy before dependency reads to reduce Figma quota and context waste
avoidNextTime: do not request full component-library descendant trees for an atomic design system

costDrivers: the R3 plan needed to incorporate the Owner-confirmed top-level Figma sections before another MCP call
avoidableCosts: future R3 reads should not infer atom molecule module hierarchy when explicit sections exist
productLessons: Figma section structure is implementation guidance and does not redefine PRD scope
technicalLessons: explicit section reads reduce payload ambiguity and make dependency mapping cheaper
designLessons: read order should follow the authored atomic-design hierarchy in the Figma file
processLessons: fold Owner-provided Figma organization updates into the read plan before calling MCP
tokenLessons: read known sections directly instead of spending context on classification inference
avoidNextTime: do not treat section naming as unknown after Owner confirms the Figma structure

costDrivers: R3 needed three MCP reads because the first read found title-case section names and the second richer read was truncated by the tool output channel
avoidableCosts: future component hierarchy reads should match section names case-insensitively and use ultra-compact output from the start
productLessons: Figma component hierarchy is implementation input and does not redefine PRD scope
technicalLessons: direct-child component maps can still exceed output limits if they include coordinates and redundant direct refs
designLessons: the component library now has an explicit Atom Molecule Module structure that supports module-first implementation
processLessons: hard-cap planning gave enough room to recover without exceeding Owner authorization
tokenLessons: reserve rich layout and descendant reads for one named module or molecule at a time
avoidNextTime: do not use rich component hierarchy output as the default R3 shape

costDrivers: R3b had to reconcile PRD work packages with the Figma module hierarchy before R4
avoidableCosts: future agents should not jump from hierarchy maps directly into broad component-detail reads
productLessons: Figma dependency routing supports implementation but does not redefine PRD scope
technicalLessons: a WP-to-module queue reduces the risk of reading low-priority component details
designLessons: module-first planning preserves the authored design-system composition
processLessons: insert no-MCP planning between expensive Figma read rounds when it materially narrows the next call
tokenLessons: local dependency planning can save multiple Figma MCP reads later
avoidNextTime: do not start R4 without a WP-to-module dependency queue

costDrivers: R2 token values needed to be reconciled with existing compatibility aliases and dark-mode overrides
avoidableCosts: future token extraction should include the target code check shape before implementation starts
productLessons: Figma tokens guide implementation but do not redefine PRD scope
technicalLessons: token reads become useful only after code variables and regression checks consume them
designLessons: page-level polish should build on a Figma-aligned token base instead of scattered local values
processLessons: no-MCP implementation slices can turn prior Figma reads into concrete project progress
tokenLessons: use completed Figma reads before requesting new Figma calls
avoidNextTime: do not leave extracted token maps as docs-only inputs

costDrivers: the first R4 module read was truncated and required one compact retry
avoidableCosts: future module contract reads should start with contract essentials only
productLessons: Figma launch module facts support PRD S1 and S16 implementation but do not redefine product scope
technicalLessons: a single module read can still exceed output limits if descendant metadata is too rich
designLessons: visible copy and Owner-confirmed role should beat stale raw Figma layer or variant names during implementation
processLessons: hard-cap planning kept the retry inside Owner-authorized budget
tokenLessons: record exact compact payload shape after truncation so the next module read can avoid repeated waste
avoidNextTime: do not repeat a truncated Figma read with the same payload shape

costDrivers: hunk isolation was needed because the module CSS already had unrelated dirty optimization-result changes
static browser visual evidence could not use file URLs and required a temporary localhost server
avoidableCosts: keep visual slices committed at clear boundaries so future hunk staging is cheaper
productLessons: R4 Figma module contracts can drive implementation proportions while PRD keeps behavior scope fixed
technicalLessons: adding launch dimensions as tokens preserved the raw-value design-system guard
designLessons: launch polish should lower recent-file hierarchy and tune proportions before adding any new visible text
processLessons: static browser screenshots are useful sanity evidence but must not replace foreground desktop-client acceptance
tokenLessons: use completed Figma reads before requesting another Figma call
avoidNextTime: do not count an empty test-name-pattern run as validation

costDrivers: three production SVGA files needed structural parsing, comparison, visual sanity checks, and PRD translation
the incident needed careful separation between generic SVGA safety and target-player-specific equivalence
avoidableCosts: future cases should use a reusable SVGA runtime-structure report command before manual analysis
productLessons: runtime structure risk must be a required short-term diagnostic and optimization lane, separate from encoded size and decoded image memory
technicalLessons: repeated frame-sequence instances can create thousands of sprites and hundreds of thousands of FrameEntity records while embedded image count stays small
designLessons: performance warnings must explain why a visually small or file-small SVGA is still risky
processLessons: real production incidents should become PRD requirements only when the cause is measurable and the mitigation path is bounded
tokenLessons: start with compact structural tables, then inspect visuals only where the anomaly appears
avoidNextTime: do not rely on image memory estimates alone for SVGA performance triage

costDrivers: Figma MCP authorization boundaries had to be preserved while still making visible progress
settings visual work needed to avoid changing theme behavior
avoidableCosts: do not spend browser setup tokens on blocked data-page preview paths
productLessons: Figma component structure is implementation evidence but not product authority
technicalLessons: settings-specific sheet tokens reduce accidental coupling to canvas mode-switch styles
designLessons: icon-led theme choices can improve settings hierarchy without adding explanatory copy
processLessons: record unresolved Figma-derived feature questions instead of silently implementing them
tokenLessons: use archived Figma screenshots for low-risk styling slices and reserve MCP reads for module contracts
avoidNextTime: do not use blocked browser data URLs for component previews

costDrivers: settings open-state regression evidence needed a new smoke artifact scenario and main-process whitelist entry
avoidableCosts: add screenshot scenarios to the artifact whitelist before the first smoke run
productLessons: regression screenshots support but do not replace Owner foreground acceptance
technicalLessons: renderer smoke captures must be paired with main-process artifact scenario validation
designLessons: settings visual polish needs open-state evidence, not just static CSS assertions
processLessons: record smoke evidence as a validation supplement without expanding product scope
tokenLessons: use existing smoke hooks before trying blocked browser preview paths
avoidNextTime: do not introduce a new smoke artifact name without updating the scenario validator

costDrivers: the new settings dialog screenshot needed to be promoted from captured artifact to proof gate
avoidableCosts: when adding smoke screenshots, update the pass condition in the same edit
productLessons: visual regression artifacts are only useful when the proof model requires the relevant state
technicalLessons: appearance screenshot proof should include the Settings open state as well as theme-specific main states
designLessons: settings polish needs evidence of the actual sheet, not only the resulting theme surfaces
processLessons: close validation gaps before moving to the next UI work package
tokenLessons: small proof-gate checks prevent later repeated explanation of the same evidence gap
avoidNextTime: do not leave captured artifacts outside the proof summary

costDrivers: the available Figma metadata tool returned right-surface symbols but not variant internals
the two-call hard cap was used to confirm the limitation
avoidableCosts: do not repeat metadata-only symbol reads when implementation needs child layout
productLessons: partial Figma evidence must not trigger product or UI implementation beyond its proof strength
technicalLessons: metadata-only component-set reads are state indexes, not pixel-level implementation contracts
designLessons: right-surface polish still needs child structure, visible text, and instance/component detail
processLessons: record partial reads honestly and stop before quota drift
tokenLessons: change read strategy after a shell-only response instead of sampling adjacent symbols
avoidNextTime: do not treat successful MCP completion as sufficient design-contract completion

costDrivers: five get_design_context reads were needed to cover the minimum WP4 dependency set
generated React Tailwind context had to be translated into implementation-neutral contracts
avoidableCosts: do not judge pixel implementation feasibility from module metadata alone
productLessons: Figma component contracts support implementation but do not expand PRD scope or visible copy
technicalLessons: get_design_context is useful for molecule and atom contracts when metadata-only module reads return shells
designLessons: WP4 right surface can now preserve two-column fact density, compact asset rows, low-emphasis tabs, and approved empty states
processLessons: pilot the first dependency before spending the whole hard cap
tokenLessons: read the smallest WP dependency set instead of a full component library
avoidNextTime: do not fall back to screenshot-only implementation before testing molecule and atom contracts

costDrivers: dev-mode Stage Manager and same-name Electron activation blocked initial screenshots
packaged app fallback was required for usable foreground evidence
parallel file changes required ownership separation
avoidableCosts: use packaged app identity first when dev-mode Electron foreground capture is ambiguous
productLessons: Figma contracts do not authorize new visible controls
technicalLessons: right-surface contracts should be enforced through tokens and design-system tests
designLessons: two-column facts and compact asset rows preserve the owner-approved information density
processLessons: do not replace foreground visual evidence with smoke evidence; use a packaged-app fallback when needed
tokenLessons: reuse saved Figma read packets for implementation and reserve new calls for missing contracts
avoidNextTime: do not retain wrong-app screenshots; delete and recapture through packaged identity

costDrivers: window size behavior crossed renderer page state and desktop host boundaries
foreground validation needed packaged app screenshots and macOS dialog automation
non-ASCII path typing failed under the active input method before switching to clipboard paste
avoidableCosts: paste real-material paths into macOS file dialogs from the clipboard instead of typing them
productLessons: full-window Launch canvas does not mean Launch should share the Preview workbench window size
technicalLessons: renderer should send semantic window modes while the host owns physical desktop dimensions
designLessons: Launch and workbench states have different spatial jobs: focused open action versus wide editing/preview workspace
processLessons: foreground packaged screenshots are required for native window-size evidence; smoke only proves regression flow
tokenLessons: record validated foreground automation recipes to avoid repeating failed dialog input attempts
avoidNextTime: do not type Chinese paths through keystroke automation in file dialogs

costDrivers: the flow had to distinguish D0 local convenience from release readiness
the script needed fail-closed handling for dirty worktrees and current-head manifest binding
avoidableCosts: future agents should call the promotion script instead of rediscovering internal package paths or manually copying app bundles
productLessons: the Product Owner quick-open app is a local D0 entry and must not be described as release acceptance
technicalLessons: local app promotion should validate package manifest, proof, app identity, archive hash, and current HEAD before replacing ~/Applications/Auto SVGA.app
processLessons: recurring owner-local setup belongs in AGENTS plus an executable script, not only chat instructions
tokenLessons: one script and one doc section reduce repeated packaging-context reloads
avoidNextTime: do not package from a dirty worktree by default
do not manually copy .app bundles into Applications

costDrivers: the small UI motion request still needed PRD and subordinate design-doc alignment
avoidableCosts: do not implement visual polish before confirming product boundary and reduced-motion expectations
productLessons: small owner-approved UI details that affect client behavior should enter the PRD authority even when they do not add a new workflow
technicalLessons: launch idle motion should be implemented as tokenized background CSS when possible, with state overrides and reduced-motion fallback
designLessons: checkerboard motion is acceptable only as low-emphasis Launch atmosphere and must not compete with Open or Drag actions
processLessons: route small UIUX changes through main PRD, subordinate design docs, review note, and worker handoff
tokenLessons: write compact requirement deltas instead of rereading or duplicating the whole short-term design system
avoidNextTime: do not let prototype-only motion become implicit product scope

costDrivers: the app icon needed stable asset storage, macOS icns generation, packager wiring, and package-resource hash validation
avoidableCosts: do not rely on local Downloads/Desktop icon paths or packager logs alone
productLessons: a temporary owner-provided icon is still a packaging contract once it affects app identity
technicalLessons: macOS app icon validation should compare packaged Contents/Resources/electron.icns hash against the repository icns
designLessons: temporary icon assets should be recorded as provisional identity, not final brand approval
processLessons: commit packaging assets separately from UI/UX implementation work
tokenLessons: inspect packager output and proof resource hashes instead of repeatedly interpreting benign compatibility warnings
avoidNextTime: do not accept a package icon change without checking the bundled resource hash

costDrivers: PM-synced Launch motion requirement required authority-doc alignment before implementation
foreground evidence was slowed by stale same-name installed app ambiguity
parallel dirty lanes required narrow ownership documentation
avoidableCosts: do not target Auto SVGA by app name when a stale installed copy can satisfy LaunchServices before the freshly packaged artifact
productLessons: small visible motion details can be implemented safely when scoped through the PRD and kept background-only
technicalLessons: Launch checker idle motion can be enforced with CSS tokens, drag pause, reduced-motion fallback, and design-system checks instead of JavaScript timers
designLessons: Optimization Result metrics should communicate before-after change with comparison hierarchy rather than generic neutral cards
processLessons: keep smoke regression, foreground evidence, and owner visual acceptance separate in the review packet
tokenLessons: switch to packaged-path foreground validation as soon as app-name activation ambiguity appears
avoidNextTime: do not retain or cite Stage Manager thumbnail screenshots as foreground evidence
do not claim Optimization Result foreground acceptance without a real foreground screenshot

costDrivers: packaged-only dependency failures require app.asar inspection and packaged binary smoke because development mode can resolve workspace dependencies
avoidableCosts: future runtime imports should update the package dependency assertion in the same patch
productLessons: client readiness requires packaged runtime dependency closure, not only source-level optimizer success
technicalLessons: .runtime/dist modules that import Node packages need explicit .runtime/node_modules copies inside app.asar
processLessons: internal packaging should fail before Owner handoff when required runtime packages are absent
tokenLessons: inspect packaged dependency closure directly instead of repeatedly diagnosing UI-level symptoms
avoidNextTime: do not rely on developer-machine module resolution as packaged App evidence

costDrivers: artifact-directory smoke passed because the app still lived under the Git checkout while the promoted installed app did not
avoidableCosts: future local-stable promotion checks should include installed-app smoke before declaring the quick-open copy ready
productLessons: local stable app readiness is stronger than package artifact readiness because the app must run outside the repository
technicalLessons: packaged apps need build identity embedded in app.asar instead of deriving headCommit from git at runtime
processLessons: treat a failed installed-app smoke as a real release-boundary bug even when artifact smoke passes
tokenLessons: verify the promoted app directly to avoid repeating path-dependent packaging assumptions
avoidNextTime: do not assume an app bundle under .artifacts behaves the same after promotion to Applications

costDrivers: fixing protobufjs revealed the next packaged-only dependency miss in fast-png once the app was tested outside the repository
avoidableCosts: scan .runtime/dist imports and package the dependency closure before the first installed-app smoke
productLessons: installed-app validation should be treated as a distinct client-readiness gate, not a repeat of artifact smoke
technicalLessons: fast-png requires fflate and iobuffer in the packaged runtime closure
processLessons: smoke diagnostics should preserve rendered failure text so package dependency misses do not collapse into generic timeouts
tokenLessons: specific failure diagnostics prevented another broad package investigation loop
avoidNextTime: do not stop after the first missing runtime package when .runtime/dist has additional external imports

costDrivers: the project already had broad review and repair rules but lacked a single owner-reported bug lifecycle
avoidableCosts: future bug handoffs should link ticket and report paths instead of copying long reproduction context between threads
productLessons: QA workflow is process authority only and must not redefine the main PRD
technicalLessons: defect closure needs separate reproduction, owner fix, and regression records
designLessons: UI/UX issues should request foreground desktop evidence when possible and treat smoke screenshots as regression-only evidence
processLessons: keep chat as transport and repository tickets as the source of truth for cross-thread bug flow
tokenLessons: role-routed, link-based tickets reduce repeated context transfer across owner threads
avoidNextTime: do not let implementation owners close their own QA tickets without regression

costDrivers: runtime text was ambiguous because SVGA dynamic text is keyed by imageKey while the UI had separate image and runtime text groups
avoidableCosts: future PRD text should specify whether a grouping rule comes from file metadata, runtime API capability, or deterministic naming semantics
productLessons: designer-named text-like raster placeholders are short-term runtime text targets when detected by deterministic key semantics
technicalLessons: text replacement classification needs explicit evidence for text, image, automatic, and ambiguous keys
designLessons: text rows should not expose image-only actions even when the target is technically an imageKey
processLessons: QA-to-PM routing is the right path when implementation cannot tell whether a behavior is a bug or unclear scope
tokenLessons: a narrow PRD clarification avoids repeated implementation and UIUX inference loops
avoidNextTime: do not describe runtime text as metadata-only when the player API actually targets imageKey

costDrivers: three owner-reported Preview bugs crossed renderer sizing, replacement byte generation, and product-model classification
the first replacement approach had to be narrowed after full tests showed it polluted a generic editor contract
avoidableCosts: write failure-first tests around both product workflow behavior and lower-level editor compatibility before moving normalization logic
productLessons: PRD S12/S13 define product Preview behavior while lower-level editor contracts remain narrower
technicalLessons: wide-canvas preview fit belongs in a pure fit model plus runtime ResizeObserver; mismatched replacement image dimensions belong in short-term workflow normalization
designLessons: text-semantic keys must leave the image action surface so designers are not shown irrelevant image-only actions
processLessons: implementation owners return Fix Ready with callback evidence and do not close QA tickets
tokenLessons: the full test suite was worth the extra run because it caught cross-lane contract drift
avoidNextTime: do not solve a short-term product policy by changing the generic SVGA resource editor

costDrivers: first real QA tickets showed that owner-thread final answers were not automatically flowing back to QA
avoidableCosts: routing prompts should define expected callback state and owner responsibility before implementation starts
technicalLessons: owner fix reports need explicit QA handoff status in addition to commit and validation details
processLessons: QA owns cross-thread follow-up and Product Owner should not be the relay for ordinary bug callbacks
tokenLessons: sync exact workflow deltas from the QA worktree rather than rewriting the full process document
avoidNextTime: do not treat an owner final answer as a completed QA handoff unless the QA thread receives the callback

costDrivers: foreground validation is useful but can interrupt the Product Owner main desktop when display placement is not planned
avoidableCosts: check display topology before foreground launch and prefer second-display or silent validation paths
technicalLessons: foreground evidence should record whether it used second display, silent fallback, or main display with reason
designLessons: foreground visual review should respect the user workspace while preserving evidence quality
processLessons: main-display foreground operation is a last resort after second-display and silent evidence paths
tokenLessons: update existing foreground-validation docs and skills instead of creating a new standalone process layer
avoidNextTime: do not launch foreground desktop apps on the main display before checking for a second display

costDrivers: the previous drag decision spec optimized for visible choice but did not reflect the owner real drag path from the macOS Dock/Desktop folder
avoidableCosts: interaction specs should define default action bias and physical drag path, not only visual split regions
productLessons: common open-new-file drops should own the center and lower-center canvas area while compare remains secondary
technicalLessons: drag overlay hit testing should verify center and lower-center points in addition to visual state
designLessons: unequal drop targets are appropriate when one action is much more frequent than the alternative
processLessons: owner daily-use interaction friction should be promoted into the PRD when it changes the interaction contract
tokenLessons: patch the main PRD plus the few conflicting UIUX docs instead of rewriting the full design package
avoidNextTime: do not use equal or left-right drop regions for a default action that users perform casually from the bottom of the screen

costDrivers: the initial top/bottom product wording defined proportions but not the exact orientation and split-line hit-test
avoidableCosts: drag-and-drop specs should always state orientation, primary default path, split boundary, and deliberate secondary action target
productLessons: Open File should own the lower primary drag zone when the Product Owner commonly drags files upward from the macOS Dock/Desktop folder
technicalLessons: runtime hit tests must prove center, lower-center, bottom-entry Open File, and top-strip Compare separately
designLessons: drop-target diagrams can invert implementation if they do not label exact top and lower regions
processLessons: when implementation returns Fix Ready under an ambiguous spec, a superseding PM contract must be routed back as a follow-up instead of silently accepting the previous orientation
tokenLessons: answering a spatial-interaction question should inspect both docs and the current implementation handoff before editing
avoidNextTime: do not leave drag target orientation implicit after changing from left-right to top-bottom

costDrivers: QA workflow changed in a separate lane and needed a concise repo-level guardrail without duplicating the detailed taxonomy
avoidableCosts: future process-policy handoffs should state whether AGENTS-level promotion is requested or only lane visibility is needed
productLessons: QA response policy is project governance and should not alter product scope or PRD requirements
processLessons: routing a QA ticket does not itself authorize interrupting the owner; handoffs need priority, importance, source, and response expectation
tokenLessons: promote only the short guardrail to AGENTS and leave detailed lifecycle rules in the QA workflow
avoidNextTime: do not use ASAP/immediate language in QA routing unless the ticket explicitly allows interruption and records why

costDrivers: parallel worker lanes can share foreground desktop state and app identity, making screenshots and QA evidence ambiguous without an explicit protocol
avoidableCosts: future owner-visible client work should name owner local stable, candidate client, worker instance, foreground lease, and baseline drift before launching foreground apps
productLessons: the installed short-term macOS app is the owner-visible baseline for QA and version progression, while PRD remains product scope authority
technicalLessons: foreground app identity needs app path, PID/process identity, window, display/workspace, and task context; app name alone is insufficient
designLessons: historical Web Preview and Workbench v1 surfaces should not steer current short-term macOS client UI unless explicitly requested
processLessons: allow concurrent desktop clients only when each worker can prove isolated instance ownership; serialize local stable replacement and ambiguous foreground work
tokenLessons: put high-level rules in AGENTS and skills, detailed rules in one engineering protocol, instead of repeating the same policy in every lane doc
avoidNextTime: do not replace /Users/huangtengxin/Applications/Auto SVGA.app if installed behavior is not represented in source, docs, review notes, manifests, or evidence

costDrivers: foreground contention includes Finder, file dialogs, After Effects, browser windows, system prompts, menu bar actions, screenshots, and clipboard operations, not only Auto SVGA client windows
avoidableCosts: define the shared foreground input channel before AEB, QA, UIUX, and short-term lanes all begin foreground automation
productLessons: foreground coordination is process governance and must not change product scope
technicalLessons: macOS foreground focus, modal dialogs, menu bar, and clipboard are global mutable resources; one active input lease at a time is safer than per-app assumptions
designLessons: second-display visual work reduces owner disruption but does not permit simultaneous keyboard/mouse/dialog automation
processLessons: A/B/C focus stealing is avoided by serializing active input, keeping leases short, and switching to non-foreground evidence when another worker is active
tokenLessons: extend the existing coordination protocol instead of creating separate AE, Finder, and client-specific policies
avoidNextTime: do not automate Finder, Open dialogs, After Effects, browser windows, or clipboard while another worker may hold foreground input

costDrivers: future VAP/Lottie/AEB/Windows work could become expensive if current short-term UI/UX work hardcodes macOS and SVGA assumptions into page modules
avoidableCosts: express future readiness as host/playback/command/error/path/dependency guardrails instead of adding premature visible features
productLessons: short-term can prepare for future multi-format routing without exposing unsupported format choices
technicalLessons: host commands, playback models, normalized inspection data, shared feedback states, and path helpers are the right seams for later platform and format migration
designLessons: resource rows, fact cells, thumbnails, replaceable rows, and empty states should be reusable while visible copy remains short-term SVGA-specific
processLessons: PM should update the nearest existing authority and architecture docs rather than create another future-format PRD
tokenLessons: targeted edits to the roadmap, UI/UX execution plan, and architecture doc avoid broad document rewrites
avoidNextTime: do not let UI cleanup accidentally create unsupported VAP, Lottie, Windows, or AEB entry points

costDrivers: owner real-use one-click optimization showed a structurally safe duplicate-merge action can still increase final file size
avoidableCosts: optimization acceptance should require net-effect deltas before implementation claims success
productLessons: an optimization result must improve its declared target metric or fail closed as no-benefit, not just produce valid SVGA bytes
technicalLessons: deduplicating encoded image resources can be offset by protobuf/zlib serialization overhead, so estimated removed-resource bytes are not enough
designLessons: result UI must distinguish successful optimization from no-benefit or negative-effect candidates
processLessons: QA should test optimization across real samples and rare synthetic candidates instead of only files known to satisfy easy optimizations
tokenLessons: a narrow PRD guardrail plus QA routing is cheaper than debugging every optimization algorithm ad hoc
avoidNextTime: do not enable Save As or Overwrite Save for one-click optimization when before/after metrics show only negative or no target benefit

costDrivers: confirmed owner requests previously had PRD updates and chat routing but no durable product delivery ticket before QA
avoidableCosts: create an ASV-REQ ticket immediately after PM evaluation and owner confirmation instead of waiting for a later status reminder
productLessons: confirmed requirements need an accountable implementation owner and QA acceptance path in addition to PRD text
processLessons: separate ASV-REQ product delivery tickets from ASV-QA defect tickets, and link QA findings back to the originating requirement
tokenLessons: a short workflow and template reduce repeated handoff explanation across PM, implementation, UIUX, and QA lanes
avoidNextTime: do not let a confirmed product requirement stop at roadmap documentation without a routed owner ticket

costDrivers: first use of the ASV-REQ process had to convert existing PRD and QA context into two durable delivery tickets
avoidableCosts: future confirmed PRD updates should create ASV-REQ tickets immediately instead of retrofitting after the fact
productLessons: optimization net-effect and runtime-structure work are confirmed product delivery items with different first owners: QA evidence first for optimization, implementation first for S17/S18
processLessons: a product delivery ticket can link existing ASV-QA evidence while still naming one accountable implementation lane
tokenLessons: referencing existing PRD anchors and QA plans avoids restating long requirement background in thread handoffs
avoidNextTime: do not wait for owner status prompts before converting confirmed PRD work into routed requirement tickets

costDrivers: runtime structure diagnostics expose low-level SVGA protocol concepts that can confuse designers and implementers if shown raw
avoidableCosts: when introducing technical metrics, define both user-facing labels and internal/report terminology in the same requirement
productLessons: right-side diagnostics should show runtime structure details with friendly labels while keeping protocol terms in technical reports
technicalLessons: SVGA SpriteEntity is a runtime playback object, not a designer/editor layer; FrameEntity is a per-frame state record
designLessons: do not blame designers with 'too many layers' when the actual risk is exported runtime object/frame-record expansion
processLessons: terminology boundaries should be clarified before short-term main starts implementation
tokenLessons: a focused PRD and requirement-ticket patch is cheaper than later UI/QA/implementation rework
avoidNextTime: do not translate sprite count as layer count in runtime-structure UI

costDrivers: runtime structure diagnostics expose low-level SVGA protocol concepts that can confuse designers and implementers if shown raw
avoidableCosts: when introducing technical metrics, define user-facing labels, internal/report terminology, calculation guidance, and disclosure policy in the same requirement
productLessons: right-side diagnostics should show runtime structure details with friendly labels while keeping protocol terms in technical reports; risk-causing fields must be visible by default
technicalLessons: SVGA SpriteEntity is a runtime playback object, not a designer/editor layer; FrameEntity is a per-frame state record
designLessons: do not blame designers with 'too many layers' when the actual risk is exported runtime object/frame-record expansion; optional detail disclosure must not hide active warnings
processLessons: terminology and disclosure boundaries should be clarified before short-term main starts implementation
tokenLessons: a focused PRD and requirement-ticket patch is cheaper than later UI/QA/implementation rework
avoidNextTime: do not translate sprite count as layer count in runtime-structure UI; do not hide risk-causing diagnostics behind more-info disclosure

costDrivers: version naming had been spread across short-term/mid-term horizons, D0/D1/D2 distribution shorthand, owner local stable app paths, and historical Workbench labels
avoidableCosts: future tickets and QA reports should record product version, release stage, distribution channel, and build identity at creation time
productLessons: use SemVer-style product lines for scope, alpha/beta/rc/stable for maturity, and channel names for package trust/audience
technicalLessons: package scripts still need a future manifest stamping step for explicit alpha.N identifiers
processLessons: do not use short-term, mid-term, D0, latest app, or Applications app as a complete version identity in handoffs
tokenLessons: a dedicated version policy plus targeted authority references avoids rewriting every historical document mention
avoidNextTime: do not route a new scope such as VAP/Lottie as Short-term Plus without assigning a product version line such as 0.2.x

costDrivers: shared dirty checkout required path-scoped review and validation
smoke screenshots expose layout regressions but still require foreground packaged-app confirmation later
avoidableCosts: record DOM overflow measurements in smoke proof when edge clipping is suspected
productLessons: runtime-structure summary is decision information and should remain visible without protocol terminology
technicalLessons: unit styling needs a narrow numeric parser so mixed diagnostic copy is not split incorrectly
compare canvases need explicit loaded-state synchronization after playback mount
designLessons: semantic fact width can vary by information type without adding new text, cards, or modules
compare empty states should reduce repeated placeholders and keep the visual decision surface calm
processLessons: batch adjacent token polish into one review and keep foreground acceptance as a separate bundled pass
tokenLessons: small UI slices should share validation runs instead of repeating smoke and review for every token edit
avoidNextTime: do not compress runtime diagnostic summaries into narrow metric cells just because other production specs are two-column facts
do not hide empty compare canvas with CSS before confirming loaded-state synchronization

costDrivers: optimization result contains required diagnostic text so visual polish cannot hide fields or rewrite product copy
avoidableCosts: keep action hierarchy tokenized so no-benefit tradeoff failed and optimized states do not need separate button systems
productLessons: net-effect rules affect visual hierarchy; disabled save actions must remain truthful without implying successful optimization
technicalLessons: shared metric value rendering prevents Preview and Optimization Result from drifting in typography and unit handling
designLessons: a primary save action plus text-weight secondary actions fits the canvas-first direction better than three similarly heavy buttons
processLessons: small style shifts still need screenshot review before continuing because token-only changes can alter perceived action priority
tokenLessons: batch visual hierarchy polish with focused tests and one smoke run; avoid opening Figma when local docs and screenshots are sufficient
avoidNextTime: do not solve optimization result density by adding unapproved explanatory text or hiding required skipped-item details

costDrivers: settings had to become lighter without adding settings explanatory text or new main-surface controls
avoidableCosts: keep appearance selector tokens separate from generic card and list tokens so it does not drift back into a card grid
productLessons: a confirmed small settings scope can still need native-feeling presentation but UI/UX must not expand the settings surface
technicalLessons: a compact segmented-control style can be achieved by retokenizing the existing component instead of changing behavior
designLessons: for simple ternary preferences a lightweight segmented control fits the canvas-first app better than large option cards
processLessons: smoke screenshot review is enough to catch gross visual regressions but final acceptance still needs packaged foreground review
tokenLessons: reuse existing smoke settings evidence instead of creating a new foreground session for every small visual density adjustment
avoidNextTime: do not add new settings or explanatory copy to make a sparse settings sheet feel fuller

costDrivers: edit reserved is intentionally sparse so polish must avoid adding fake controls or explanatory text
avoidableCosts: do not judge the full three-column edit layout from the 720x720 smoke screenshot
productLessons: a reserved mode can still need precise component tokens without implying unsupported editing capability
technicalLessons: LayerRow should own row density and thumbnail size instead of reusing heavier generic resource-row proportions
designLessons: lower typography and thumbnail weight is safer short-term polish than adding selection states cards or placeholder copy
processLessons: record smoke viewport evidence limitations immediately to avoid repeated false layout findings
tokenLessons: before changing a sparse reserved state verify whether the issue is real layout or a smoke viewport artifact
avoidNextTime: do not use square smoke screenshots as final evidence for wide Preview Edit or Compare layout acceptance

costDrivers: nonforeground evidence needed a wider scenario before Figma-to-code visual comparison could be judged reliably
shared dirty checkout required path-scoped staging
avoidableCosts: new product smoke scenarios should update renderer capture host allowlist sizing and tests together before the first full smoke run
productLessons: wide smoke evidence supports the design verification gate but does not replace foreground packaged-app acceptance
technicalLessons: host scenario allowlists are part of the screenshot contract and should be asserted beside runner capture calls
designLessons: Preview visual polish should be judged at workbench ratio instead of only from square launch-sized artifacts
processLessons: separate evidence infrastructure from visible polish so visual changes can be reviewed against a stable baseline
tokenLessons: one wide baseline artifact can prevent repeated manual debate about layout proportions across later WPs
avoidNextTime: do not add a smoke artifact scenario only on the renderer side

costDrivers: visual scale issue only became obvious after adding a wide workbench screenshot
shared fit model touches Preview Compare and Optimization playback surfaces
avoidableCosts: separate internal canvas resolution from CSS display size before changing visual scale tokens
productLessons: default Preview should not introduce zoom-like behavior without explicit product scope
technicalLessons: playback fit needs source-size caps so responsive downscaling does not become silent upscaling
designLessons: canvas-first immersion depends on quiet space around artwork, not making every file fill the available workbench
processLessons: use wide smoke evidence to find visual scale issues before foreground packaged-app sessions
tokenLessons: model-level tests are cheaper than repeatedly inspecting screenshots for every source-size scenario
avoidNextTime: do not judge artwork scale from launch-sized or square screenshots

costDrivers: playback sketches imply controls that are not implemented in the current short-term slice
shared playback bar appears in Preview and Compare states
avoidableCosts: use tokenized spacing to align existing controls instead of adding unsupported buttons
productLessons: design references must not be used to silently create new interaction scope
technicalLessons: playback bar grid proportions should be asserted in tests so time text does not drift to the canvas edge again
designLessons: quiet trailing space is preferable to false affordances when future controls are absent
processLessons: wide evidence should drive small visible polish slices but each slice still needs smoke and review
tokenLessons: component-level fractional layout tokens are cheaper than page-specific media-query overrides
avoidNextTime: do not fill visual gaps by inventing loop fullscreen or zoom controls without scope

costDrivers: right-surface token polish had to stay within existing Figma contracts and product-approved visible surfaces
shared dirty checkout required path-scoped staging
avoidableCosts: check existing Figma packets before making another MCP read for a small token-level mismatch
productLessons: visual fidelity work should not add new right-panel copy or controls when the product model is already defined
technicalLessons: right-surface density can be improved through component tokens and token assertions without touching renderer data flow
designLessons: selected tabs and fact grids can feel more native by weakening rings and using Figma-recorded spacing rather than adding separators
processLessons: use existing read packets first and reserve fresh Figma calls for unresolved visible mismatches
tokenLessons: small token-only WPs are cheap when prior Figma packets contain exact component contracts
avoidNextTime: do not spend Figma MCP budget before checking whether the target token contract is already archived
